calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition To 1)Confirm Validity Of Advance Health Car Directive; And 2)Determine That Act Of Attorney In Fact Denying Caregiver Access To Principal
Off Calendar May-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Weinberg for and with petitioner Debra Sabahi; Heather Campoliano (by CourtCall) and Eli Coffino for respondent Annaliese Kelleher; Steven Gould specially appearing for James Kelleher.

All present agree that there is no dispute that the advanced health care directive is valid, and all further agree that Debra Dolch was appointed and taken charge of the situation, and the parties are following her recommendations. Mr. Coffino confired that a geriatric case manager has now been appointed and is managing the care for Mr. Kelleher. Mr. Weinberg said that he is happy to state that over the past two months things have stabilized, and that in addition to the order confirming the validity of the health care directive he was now seeking only two things: prohibitioon of the use of tobacco and alcohol in the home. Mr. Coffino responded that things are going sommthly now under the direction of Debra Dolch, and that he did not see the need to change anything. Mr. Gould corcurred that things have calmed down now and peace had been obtained, and stated his belief that the Court should not intervene further at this time. Mr. Weinstein reiterated his request for an order confirming the validity of the health care directive, permitting the filing of medical records under seal, and restricting smoking, drinking of alcohol, and the administration of medicine. The Court stated the he would confirm the validity of the health csare directive, and requested a revised order granting the petition in part and denying it in part, for the Court's review. The petition was granted in part and denied in part, subject to receipt and review of a revised order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)
